qas.q: What does theacronym HIPAA stand for?
qas.a: Health Insurance Portability Accountability Act

qas.q: Patient's are given a copy of this explanation of privacy practices at their first contact?
qas.a: Notice of Privacy Practice

qas.q: Who should a patient contact if they observe privacy problems in their providers offices?
qas.a: Privacy Officer

qas.q: Who should a patient contact if they observe privacy problems in their providers offices?
qas.a: Office of Civil Rights

qas.q: Who should a patient contact if they observe privacy problems in their providers offices?
qas.a: Department Of Human Service

qas.q: What year was HIPAA established?
What is HIPAA?
qas.a: A group of laws designed to protect medical records and other health information.

qas.q: Name one benefit of HIPAA privacy rule:
qas.a: Patients have more control over their medical records.

qas.q: Name one benefit of HIPAA privacy rule:
qas.a: Patients are able to make informed choices regarding how their personal health is used.

qas.q: Name one benefit of HIPAA privacy rule:
qas.a: Boundaries are set on the use and release of health records.

qas.q: Name one benefit of HIPAA privacy rule:
qas.a: Safeguards are established that healthcare providers must achieve to protect the privacy of health information.

qas.q: What does Title 1 provide for?
qas.a: It regulates insurance reform.

qas.q: What does Title II provide for?
qas.a: It deals with administratice simplification.

qas.q: Name one patients' rights under HIPAA:
qas.a: The right to notice of a facility's privacy practice.

qas.q: Name one patients' rights under HIPAA:
qas.a: The right to have access to view and obtain a copy of their PHI.

qas.q: Name one patients' rights under HIPAA:
qas.a: The right to restrict certain parts or uses of their PHI.

qas.q: Name one patients' rights under HIPAA:
qas.a: The right to request that communications from the facility be kept confidential.

qas.q: Name one guideline that you can follow in the medical office to assure patient privacy
qas.a: Consider the conversations occuring throughout the office could be overheard.

qas.q: Name one guideline that you can follow in the medical office to assure patient privacy
qas.a: Keep user names and passwords confidential and change them often.

qas.q: Name one guideline that you can follow in the medical office to assure patient privacy
qas.a: Send all privacy related questions or concerns to the appropriate staff members.

qas.q: Name one guideline that you can follow in the medical office to assure patient privacy
qas.a: Direct all questions to the supervisors or Policy Officers.

qas.q: Name one guideline that you can follow in the medical office to assure patient privacy
qas.a: Immediately report any suspected or known improper behavior to supevisors or the Policy officers.

qas.q: Describe what might be considered an accidental disclosure
qas.a: Physicians speaking with patients in semi-private hospital rooms.
